name: Colorectal Cancer
summary: Colorectal cancer arises from colon/rectum epithelium and involves hereditary syndromes and polygenic background. GENARCH provides educational population-level summaries.
adolescent_relevance: Rare in adolescence; hereditary syndromes illustrate mechanism and inheritance in an educational framing.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Diet patterns influence metabolic/inflammatory context in cohorts.
Microbiome patterns correlate with CRC; causality is complex.
Alcohol is associated with cancer outcomes in population studies.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: CRC GWAS have been European-ancestry dominated historically; representation improving.
population_equity.transferability_notes: PRS performance varies by ancestry and screening context.
population_equity.data_gaps: Microbiome study heterogeneity and confounding limit inference.

references.title: Hereditary colorectal cancer syndromes: APC and mismatch repair pathways (review)
Gut microbiome and colorectal cancer: evidence overview (review)
Alcohol consumption and cancer risk: evidence synthesis (review)
